---
title: "exercise3"
author: "cden903"
date: "2026-03-17"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{R}
# Load the dataset
camplake <- read.csv("camplake.csv")

# Fit the linear model: Length as response, Age and Scale.Radius as predictors
m <- lm(Length ~ Age + Scale.Radius, data = camplake)


# Set up a 1x2 plotting area
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# Residuals vs Age
plot(camplake$Age, residuals(m), 
     main = "Residuals vs Age", xlab = "Age", ylab = "Residuals")
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)

# Residuals vs Scale.Radius
plot(camplake$Scale.Radius, residuals(m), 
     main = "Residuals vs Scale.Radius", xlab = "Scale Radius", ylab = "Residuals")
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lty = 2)

par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# Plot for Age
plot(camplake$Age, camplake$Length, main = "Fit: Age vs Length", 
     xlab = "Age", ylab = "Length", pch = 16)
points(camplake$Age, predict(m), col = "blue", pch = 4)

# Plot for Scale.Radius
plot(camplake$Scale.Radius, camplake$Length, main = "Fit: Scale.Radius vs Length", 
     xlab = "Scale Radius", ylab = "Length", pch = 16)
points(camplake$Scale.Radius, predict(m), col = "red", pch = 4)


par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# Q-Q plot for Predictions
qqnorm(predict(m), main = "Normal Q-Q (Predictions)")
qqline(predict(m), col = "steelblue", lwd = 2)

# Q-Q plot for Residuals
qqnorm(residuals(m), main = "Normal Q-Q (Residuals)")
qqline(residuals(m), col = "steelblue", lwd = 2)

# Reset plotting layout to see them one by one or in a grid
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(m)


```

```{R}
# Create a sequence of x values from 0 to 2*pi
x <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 1000)

# Calculate the sine of those values
y <- sin(x)

# Create the plot
plot(x, y, 
     type = "l", 
     col = "#C0C0C0",       # This is the Hex code for Silver
     lwd = 3, 
     main = "Sine Curve on [0, 2pi]",
     xlab = "x", 
     ylab = "sin(x)")


```
